title: crepis virens var. linifolia
intro: Crepis virens var. linifolia, commonly known as the hairy hawk's beard or narrow-leaved hawk's beard, is a plant species in the dandelion family (Asteraceae) that is native to North America. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to soothe digestive issues and reduce fever. The health benefits of Crepis virens var. linifolia include its potential to aid in the treatment of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its ability to support the immune system. Crepis virens var. linifolia can be prepared as a tea or infusion by steeping the leaves and flowers in hot water, and it can also be used in tincture or capsule form to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
